channel.title: Series on NullDeref
channel.description: Recent content in Series on NullDeref
channel.copyright: See this site's source code [on GitHub](https://github.com/marioortizmanero/nullderef.com), licensed under GPLv3 ·

item.title: Get off your phone!
item.description: My journey learning more about how to reduce phone usage, and eventually building something. It's as complicated as fun to solve!

item.title: Writing A Plugin System in Rust
item.description: My [LFX mentorship](https://mentorship.lfx.linuxfoundation.org/project/b90f7174-fc53-40bc-b9e2-9905f88c38ff) with [Tremor](https://www.tremor.rs/), in which I implement a Plugin System to maximize modularity and reduce Rust's wild compilation times. This is done with [dynamic loading](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dynamic_loading), but its alternatives are also covered in detail in the first articles.

item.title: Maintaining RSpotify
item.description: I was a contributor to [the RSpotify library](https://github.com/ramsayleung/rspotify) to sharpen my Rust skills. This crate provides easy-to-use and complete bindings for [the Spotify web API](https://developer.spotify.com/documentation/web-api). I tried to improve it in all sorts of ways, such as reducing bloat, adding features, or improving its performance. You can read more about them in this series.
